papers.title: PixelSmile: Toward Fine-Grained Facial Expression Editing
papers.abstract: Fine-grained facial expression editing has long been limited by intrinsic semantic overlap. To address this, we construct the Flex Facial Expression (FFE) dataset with continuous affective annotations and establish FFE-Bench to evaluate structural confusion, editing accuracy, linear controllability, and the trade-off between expression editing and identity preservation. We propose PixelSmile, a diffusion framework that disentangles expression semantics via fully symmetric joint training. PixelSmile combines intensity supervision with contrastive learning to produce stronger and more distinguishable expressions, achieving precise and stable linear expression control through textual latent interpolation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that PixelSmile achieves superior disentanglement and robust identity preservation, confirming its effectiveness for continuous, controllable, and fine-grained expression editing, while naturally supporting smooth expression blending.

structured_summary.chinese_title: PixelSmile: 朝向细粒度面部表情编辑
structured_summary.chinese_summary: PixelSmile 通过文本潜在空间插值实现精确且稳定的线性表情控制。
structured_summary.pain_point: 细粒度面部表情编辑受限于内在语义重叠。
structured_summary.core_value: 通过对比学习和强度监督实现更强的表情控制，支持平滑的表情过渡。
structured_summary.technical_architecture: PixelSmile 采用对称联合训练和文本潜在空间插值。
structured_summary.limitations: 未来需探索更多表情类型的应用。
key_contributions: 构建了连续情感标注的 Flex Facial Expression (FFE) 数据集
提出了 PixelSmile 差分框架，实现表情语义分离
通过文本潜在空间插值实现精确的线性表情控制
application_scenarios: 虚拟现实中的情感表达
technical_highlights: 结合强度监督与对比学习
实现精确且稳定的线性表情控制

papers.title: Intern-S1-Pro: Scientific Multimodal Foundation Model at Trillion Scale
papers.abstract: We introduce Intern-S1-Pro, the first one-trillion-parameter scientific multimodal foundation model. Scaling to this unprecedented size, the model delivers a comprehensive enhancement across both general and scientific domains. Beyond stronger reasoning and image-text understanding capabilities, its intelligence is augmented with advanced agent capabilities. Simultaneously, its scientific expertise has been vastly expanded to master over 100 specialized tasks across critical science fields, including chemistry, materials, life sciences, and earth sciences. Achieving this massive scale is made possible by the robust infrastructure support of XTuner and LMDeploy, which facilitates highly efficient Reinforcement Learning (RL) training at the 1-trillion parameter level while ensuring strict precision consistency between training and inference. By seamlessly integrating these advancements, Intern-S1-Pro further fortifies the fusion of general and specialized intelligence, working as a Specializable Generalist, demonstrating its position in the top tier of open-source models for general capabilities, while outperforming proprietary models in the depth of specialized scientific tasks.

structured_summary.chinese_title: Intern-S1-Pro：万亿参数的科学多模态基础模型
structured_summary.chinese_summary: Intern-S1-Pro 是首个万亿参数的科学多模态基础模型，显著提升了科学和通用领域的综合能力。
structured_summary.pain_point: 现有模型在处理大规模科学任务时存在性能瓶颈。
structured_summary.core_value: 通过大规模训练，模型在科学和通用领域均表现出色，具备更强的推理和多模态理解能力。
structured_summary.technical_architecture: 采用了 XTuner 和 LMDeploy 的基础设施支持，实现了高效的强化学习训练。
structured_summary.limitations: 未来需要进一步优化模型的泛化能力和效率。
key_contributions: 实现首个万亿参数的科学多模态基础模型
显著提升科学和通用领域的综合能力
增强了模型的推理和多模态理解能力
application_scenarios: 科学领域的专业任务处理
通用领域的多模态理解
technical_highlights: 采用了高效的强化学习训练方法
确保了训练和推理的精度一致性

papers.title: RealRestorer: Towards Generalizable Real-World Image Restoration with Large-Scale Image Editing Models
papers.abstract: Image restoration under real-world degradations is critical for downstream tasks such as autonomous driving and object detection. However, existing restoration models are often limited by the scale and distribution of their training data, resulting in poor generalization to real-world scenarios. Recently, large-scale image editing models have shown strong generalization ability in restoration tasks, especially for closed-source models like Nano Banana Pro, which can restore images while preserving consistency. Nevertheless, achieving such performance with those large universal models requires substantial data and computational costs. To address this issue, we construct a large-scale dataset covering nine common real-world degradation types and train a state-of-the-art open-source model to narrow the gap with closed-source alternatives. Furthermore, we introduce RealIR-Bench, which contains 464 real-world degraded images and tailored evaluation metrics focusing on degradation removal and consistency preservation. Extensive experiments demonstrate our model ranks first among open-source methods, achieving state-of-the-art performance.

structured_summary.chinese_title: RealRestorer: 针对大规模现实世界图像恢复的通用图像修复模型
structured_summary.chinese_summary: 提出RealIR-Bench基准，构建大规模现实世界降级数据集，训练开源模型，实现开放源代码方法的领先性能。
structured_summary.pain_point: 现有图像修复模型在现实世界场景中的泛化能力不足。
structured_summary.core_value: 通过大规模现实世界降级数据集和开源模型，提高图像修复的现实世界泛化能力。
structured_summary.technical_architecture: 构建大规模现实世界降级数据集并训练先进开源模型。
structured_summary.quantitative_results: 实验表明，模型在开放源代码方法中排名第一，达到领先性能。
structured_summary.competitors: 对比Nano Banana Pro等闭源模型。
structured_summary.limitations: 需要大量数据和计算资源。
key_contributions: 构建大规模现实世界降级数据集
提出RealIR-Bench基准
训练开源模型实现领先性能

papers.title: Calibri: Enhancing Diffusion Transformers via Parameter-Efficient Calibration
papers.abstract: In this paper, we uncover the hidden potential of Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) to significantly enhance generative tasks. Through an in-depth analysis of the denoising process, we demonstrate that introducing a single learned scaling parameter can significantly improve the performance of DiT blocks. Building on this insight, we propose Calibri, a parameter-efficient approach that optimally calibrates DiT components to elevate generative quality. Calibri frames DiT calibration as a black-box reward optimization problem, which is efficiently solved using an evolutionary algorithm and modifies just ~100 parameters. Experimental results reveal that despite its lightweight design, Calibri consistently improves performance across various text-to-image models. Notably, Calibri also reduces the inference steps required for image generation, all while maintaining high-quality outputs.

structured_summary.chinese_title: Calibri：通过参数高效校准增强扩散变换器
structured_summary.chinese_summary: 通过引入单个学习缩放参数，Calibri显著提升了扩散变换器的生成任务性能。
structured_summary.pain_point: 扩散变换器在生成任务中的性能提升空间。
structured_summary.core_value: 通过少量参数优化显著提升生成质量，减少推理步骤。
structured_summary.technical_architecture: 将扩散变换器校准作为黑盒奖励优化问题，使用进化算法解决。
structured_summary.quantitative_results: 实验结果显示Calibri在多种文本到图像模型中持续提升性能。
structured_summary.limitations: 未来可探索更多应用场景。
key_contributions: 提出Calibri方法优化扩散变换器
通过少量参数显著提升生成质量

papers.title: MACRO: Advancing Multi-Reference Image Generation with Structured Long-Context Data
papers.abstract: Generating images conditioned on multiple visual references is critical for real-world applications such as multi-subject composition, narrative illustration, and novel view synthesis, yet current models suffer from severe performance degradation as the number of input references grows. We identify the root cause as a fundamental data bottleneck: existing datasets are dominated by single- or few-reference pairs and lack the structured, long-context supervision needed to learn dense inter-reference dependencies. To address this, we introduce MacroData, a large-scale dataset of 400K samples, each containing up to 10 reference images, systematically organized across four complementary dimensions -- Customization, Illustration, Spatial reasoning, and Temporal dynamics -- to provide comprehensive coverage of the multi-reference generation space. Recognizing the concurrent absence of standardized evaluation protocols, we further propose MacroBench, a benchmark of 4,000 samples that assesses generative coherence across graded task dimensions and input scales. Extensive experiments show that fine-tuning on MacroData yields substantial improvements in multi-reference generation, and ablation studies further reveal synergistic benefits of cross-task co-training and effective strategies for handling long-context complexity. The dataset and benchmark will be publicly released.

structured_summary.chinese_title: MACRO: 提升多参考图像生成的结构化长上下文数据
structured_summary.chinese_summary: 提出MacroData和MacroBench，显著提升多参考图像生成性能。
structured_summary.pain_point: 现有模型在处理多个参考图像时性能大幅下降。
structured_summary.core_value: 提供大规模结构化长上下文数据，增强模型对多参考图像的理解。
structured_summary.technical_architecture: 构建MacroData和MacroBench，系统性地组织多参考图像数据。
structured_summary.quantitative_results: 实验表明，基于MacroData的微调显著提升多参考生成效果。
structured_summary.limitations: 未来需探索更多复杂场景的应用。
key_contributions: 提出大规模多参考图像生成数据集MacroData
构建全面的基准测试MacroBench
显著提升多参考图像生成性能
technical_highlights: 系统性组织多参考图像数据
提供标准化的评估基准

papers.title: Voxtral TTS
papers.abstract: We introduce Voxtral TTS, an expressive multilingual text-to-speech model that generates natural speech from as little as 3 seconds of reference audio. Voxtral TTS adopts a hybrid architecture that combines auto-regressive generation of semantic speech tokens with flow-matching for acoustic tokens. These tokens are encoded and decoded with Voxtral Codec, a speech tokenizer trained from scratch with a hybrid VQ-FSQ quantization scheme. In human evaluations conducted by native speakers, Voxtral TTS is preferred for multilingual voice cloning due to its naturalness and expressivity, achieving a 68.4\% win rate over ElevenLabs Flash v2.5. We release the model weights under a CC BY-NC license.

structured_summary.chinese_summary: Voxtral TTS 是一种多语言文本转语音模型，仅需 3 秒参考音频即可生成自然语音。
structured_summary.pain_point: 现有 TTS 模型需要大量参考音频，生成的语音自然度和表达能力有限。
structured_summary.core_value: Voxtral TTS 通过混合架构提高了语音的自然度和表达能力，适用于多语言语音克隆。
structured_summary.technical_architecture: 采用混合架构结合自回归生成语义语音令牌和流匹配生成声学令牌。
structured_summary.quantitative_results: 在人类评估中，Voxtral TTS 的自然度和表达能力优于 ElevenLabs Flash v2.5，胜率 68.4%。
structured_summary.limitations: 未来需要进一步提高模型的鲁棒性和泛化能力。
key_contributions: 提出了一种新的混合架构
使用 VQ-FSQ 量化方案训练语音令牌
technical_highlights: 自回归生成语义语音令牌

papers.title: SlopCodeBench: Benchmarking How Coding Agents Degrade Over Long-Horizon Iterative Tasks
papers.abstract: Software development is iterative, yet agentic coding benchmarks overwhelmingly evaluate single-shot solutions against complete specifications. Code can pass the test suite but become progressively harder to extend. Recent iterative benchmarks attempt to close this gap, but constrain the agent's design decisions too tightly to faithfully measure how code quality shapes future extensions. We introduce SlopCodeBench, a language-agnostic benchmark comprising 20 problems and 93 checkpoints, in which agents repeatedly extend their own prior solutions under evolving specifications that force architectural decisions without prescribing internal structure. We track two trajectory-level quality signals: verbosity, the fraction of redundant or duplicated code, and structural erosion, the share of complexity mass concentrated in high-complexity functions. No agent solves any problem end-to-end across 11 models; the highest checkpoint solve rate is 17.2%. Quality degrades steadily: erosion rises in 80% of trajectories and verbosity in 89.8%. Against 48 open-source Python repositories, agent code is 2.2x more verbose and markedly more eroded. Tracking 20 of those repositories over time shows that human code stays flat, while agent code deteriorates with each iteration. A prompt-intervention study shows that initial quality can be improved, but it does not halt degradation. These results demonstrate that pass-rate benchmarks systematically undermeasure extension robustness, and that current agents lack the design discipline iterative software development demands.

structured_summary.chinese_title: SlopCodeBench: 评估编码代理在长期迭代任务中退化情况的基准测试
structured_summary.chinese_summary: 引入SlopCodeBench基准测试，评估编码代理在长期迭代任务中的代码质量退化情况。
structured_summary.pain_point: 现有基准测试无法准确衡量代码质量对后续扩展的影响。
structured_summary.core_value: 通过动态演化规格强制架构决策，评估代码质量对长期扩展的影响。
structured_summary.technical_architecture: 包含20个问题和93个检查点的多阶段扩展基准测试。
structured_summary.limitations: 仅部分问题被解决，未来需扩展更多问题。
key_contributions: 引入SlopCodeBench基准测试
评估代码质量对长期扩展的影响

application_scenarios: 评估编码代理在长期迭代任务中的表现
优化代码质量和可扩展性

papers.title: MSA: Memory Sparse Attention for Efficient End-to-End Memory Model Scaling to 100M Tokens
papers.abstract:
Long-term memory is a cornerstone of human intelligence. Enabling AI to process lifetime-scale information remains a long-standing pursuit in
  the field. Due to the constraints of full-attention architectures, the effective context length of large language models (LLMs) is typically
  limited to 1M tokens. Existing approaches, such as hybrid linear attention, fixed-size memory states (e.g., RNNs), and external storage
  methods like RAG or agent systems, attempt to extend this limit. However, they often suffer from severe precision degradation and rapidly
  increasing latency as context length grows, an inability to dynamically modify memory content, or a lack of end-to-end optimization. These
  bottlenecks impede complex scenarios like large-corpus summarization, Digital Twins, and long-history agent reasoning, while limiting memory
  capacity and slowing inference. We present Memory Sparse Attention (MSA), an end-to-end trainable, efficient, and massively scalable memory
  model framework. Through core innovations including scalable sparse attention and document-wise RoPE, MSA achieves linear complexity in both
  training and inference while maintaining exceptional stability, exhibiting less than 9% degradation when scaling from 16K to 100M tokens.
  Furthermore, KV cache compression, combined with Memory Parallel, enables 100M-token inference on 2xA800 GPUs. We also propose Memory
  Interleaving to facilitate complex multi-hop reasoning across scattered memory segments. MSA significantly surpasses frontier LLMs,
  state-of-the-art RAG systems, and leading memory agents in long-context benchmarks. These results demonstrate that by decoupling memory
  capacity from reasoning, MSA provides a scalable foundation to endow general-purpose models with intrinsic, lifetime-scale memory.

structured_summary.chinese_title: MSA: 记忆稀疏注意力机制以高效扩展至100M标记的端到端记忆模型
structured_summary.chinese_summary: MSA通过稀疏注意力和文档级RoPE实现高效、可扩展的记忆模型。
structured_summary.pain_point: 现有方法在处理长上下文时存在精度下降、延迟增加、无法动态修改记忆内容等问题。
structured_summary.core_value: MSA提供了一种高效、可扩展且端到端的记忆模型框架，适用于大规模场景。
structured_summary.technical_architecture: MSA采用可扩展的稀疏注意力和文档级RoPE技术。
structured_summary.limitations: 未来需要进一步验证其在更广泛场景下的适用性。
key_contributions: 提出了一种新的稀疏注意力机制
引入了文档级RoPE技术
实现了线性复杂度的训练和推理

papers.title: AVControl: Efficient Framework for Training Audio-Visual Controls
papers.abstract: Controlling video and audio generation requires diverse modalities, from depth and pose to camera trajectories and audio transformations, yet existing approaches either train a single monolithic model for a fixed set of controls or introduce costly architectural changes for each new modality. We introduce AVControl, a lightweight, extendable framework built on LTX-2, a joint audio-visual foundation model, where each control modality is trained as a separate LoRA on a parallel canvas that provides the reference signal as additional tokens in the attention layers, requiring no architectural changes beyond the LoRA adapters themselves. We show that simply extending image-based in-context methods to video fails for structural control, and that our parallel canvas approach resolves this. On the VACE Benchmark, we outperform all evaluated baselines on depth- and pose-guided generation, inpainting, and outpainting, and show competitive results on camera control and audio-visual benchmarks. Our framework supports a diverse set of independently trained modalities: spatially-aligned controls such as depth, pose, and edges, camera trajectory with intrinsics, sparse motion control, video editing, and, to our knowledge, the first modular audio-visual controls for a joint generation model. Our method is both compute- and data-efficient: each modality requires only a small dataset and converges within a few hundred to a few thousand training steps, a fraction of the budget of monolithic alternatives. We publicly release our code and trained LoRA checkpoints.

structured_summary.chinese_title: AVControl: 高效的音频-视觉控制训练框架
structured_summary.chinese_summary: AVControl 提供了一种无需修改架构即可扩展多种控制模态的方法。
structured_summary.pain_point: 现有方法要么训练单一模型，要么为每种新模态引入昂贵的架构变更。
structured_summary.core_value: 通过轻量级的 LoRA 方法和并行画布，AVControl 支持多种独立训练的控制模态。
structured_summary.technical_architecture: 基于 LTX-2 基础模型，AVControl 使用 LoRA 训练每个控制模态。
structured_summary.quantitative_results: 在 VACE 基准上，AVControl 在深度和姿态引导生成、修复和扩展方面优于所有基线。
structured_summary.competitors: 对比了多种基线方法，包括单一模型和针对特定模态的模型。
structured_summary.limitations: 未来工作可能包括扩展更多模态和优化训练效率。
key_contributions: 无需修改架构即可扩展多种控制模态
并行画布方法解决结构控制问题
application_scenarios: 深度和姿态引导的视频生成

papers.title: Less Gaussians, Texture More: 4K Feed-Forward Textured Splatting
papers.abstract: Existing feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting methods predict pixel-aligned primitives, leading to a quadratic growth in primitive count as resolution increases. This fundamentally limits their scalability, making high-resolution synthesis such as 4K intractable. We introduce LGTM (Less Gaussians, Texture More), a feed-forward framework that overcomes this resolution scaling barrier. By predicting compact Gaussian primitives coupled with per-primitive textures, LGTM decouples geometric complexity from rendering resolution. This approach enables high-fidelity 4K novel view synthesis without per-scene optimization, a capability previously out of reach for feed-forward methods, all while using significantly fewer Gaussian primitives. Project page: https://yxlao.github.io/lgtm/

structured_summary.chinese_title: 少高斯，多纹理：4K 前馈纹理斑点绘制
structured_summary.chinese_summary: 通过预测紧凑的高斯原语并结合每个原语的纹理，LGTM 实现了高保真 4K 新视图合成。
structured_summary.pain_point: 现有前馈 3D 高斯斑点绘制方法在分辨率增加时导致原语数量呈二次增长，限制了其可扩展性。
structured_summary.core_value: LGTM 通过分离几何复杂度和渲染分辨率，显著减少了高斯原语的数量，同时保持高保真度。
structured_summary.technical_architecture: 预测紧凑的高斯原语并结合每个原语的纹理，实现高分辨率合成。
structured_summary.limitations: 未来工作可能涉及更广泛的场景适应性。
key_contributions: 提出 LGTM 框架
减少高斯原语数量
实现高保真 4K 新视图合成
